summary: Market Intelligence redesign fully tested. All 15 backend API tests passed. All 14 frontend UI tests passed. The new Bloomberg-style Market page includes: KPI cards (Collection, Revenue, Profit, Avg Sale), Sales Performance area chart, Your Collection sidebar with clickable items, My Watchlist with add/remove/lookup functionality, Hot on the Market trending cards, Monthly Performance bar chart, and big search bar with price lookup. Price lookup returns real eBay sold data via Jina Reader API with median prices, sales count, and recent comparable sales.
passed_tests: BACKEND - GET /api/market/hot-cards returns 200 with trending cards (Victor Wembanyama, LeBron James, Luka Doncic, Anthony Edwards)
BACKEND - GET /api/market/portfolio-health returns 200 with inventory items and sport detection
BACKEND - GET /api/market/watchlist returns 200 with items list
BACKEND - POST /api/market/watchlist adds player to watchlist
BACKEND - POST /api/market/watchlist adds card to watchlist
BACKEND - POST /api/market/watchlist with empty name returns 400
BACKEND - POST + GET verifies item appears in watchlist after add
BACKEND - DELETE /api/market/watchlist/{name} removes item and verifies removal
BACKEND - GET /api/market/card-value returns market data with data_source sold/active
BACKEND - GET /api/market/card-value with PSA 10 query detects grade correctly
BACKEND - GET /api/dashboard/analytics returns sales data with total_revenue, total_profit, total_orders
BACKEND - Hot cards structure validation (name, query, sport, tag fields)
BACKEND - Hot cards returns user_sports based on inventory
BACKEND - Portfolio health has sport auto-detection
BACKEND - Portfolio health totals are numeric
FRONTEND - Market page loads with data-testid='market-page'
FRONTEND - KPI card: Collection (3 cards, $1.9k invested)
FRONTEND - KPI card: Revenue ($587.53, 16 total sales)
FRONTEND - KPI card: Profit ($498.37, $89.16 in fees)
FRONTEND - KPI card: Avg Sale ($36.72, Best: $90.50)
FRONTEND - Sales Performance area chart renders with cumulative data
FRONTEND - Your Collection sidebar shows 3 items (Kobe Bryant $1.5k, Michael Jordan $350, Kobe Bryant SkyBox)
FRONTEND - Hot on the Market shows 4 trending cards (all Basketball)
FRONTEND - Clicking hot card (Victor Wembanyama) triggers search and displays results
FRONTEND - Price lookup result shows: big median ($87.50), sales count (12), range, comparable sales
FRONTEND - 'View all on eBay' link present in results
FRONTEND - My Watchlist section with add (+) button
FRONTEND - Add to watchlist form: name input, player/card type toggle, save button
FRONTEND - Added item appears in watchlist with Lookup and X buttons
FRONTEND - Lookup button on watchlist item triggers search
FRONTEND - X button removes item from watchlist (verified with toast)
FRONTEND - Search bar accepts input and Search button triggers lookup
FRONTEND - Portfolio item click triggers price lookup search
FRONTEND - Monthly Performance bar chart with Revenue/Profit legend
critical_code_review_comments: MarketModule.jsx properly uses Recharts (AreaChart, BarChart) for visualizations
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes for testing
API calls use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from environment correctly
Watchlist CRUD operations work with MongoDB collection 'market_watchlist'
Hot cards are curated based on user's inventory sports (not real-time scraped)
Price lookup uses Jina Reader API - may take 5-15 seconds for response
Grade detection regex correctly identifies PSA/BGS/SGC grades in queries

success_rate.frontend: 100% (14/14 UI elements verified, all interactions working)

seed_data_creation: None - test items cleaned up after testing (TEST_ prefixed watchlist items)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Market Intelligence redesign fully tested. Uses Recharts for charts, Jina Reader API for eBay sold data scraping (5-15 sec latency). Hot cards are curated per sport, not live scraped. Watchlist stored in MongoDB 'market_watchlist' collection. All data-testid attributes present for test automation. Portfolio items clickable for instant price lookup. Test data prefixed with TEST_ for easy cleanup.
